1→# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

    11→
    12→1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - EMERGENCIAL)
    13→   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
    14→   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
    15→   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas
    16→   - Auditar código, documentar usos, migrar estados críticos
    17→   - Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra contra React hooks para estado crítico
    18→
    19→2. **PERF-001** (Priority 0)
    20→   - Adicionar ferramenta de métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real
    21→   - Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada), stats-gl, stats.js
    22→   - Painel deve mostrar: FPS, MS, MB, GPU time, draw calls, triangles
    23→   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)
    24→   - Toggle para mostrar/esconder (tecla 'P' ou botão)
    25→   - **LOGGING DETALHADO** (adicionado após revisão):
    26→     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
    27→     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console
    28→     - window.__PERF_EXPORT__() para exportar log completo
    29→     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()
    30→     - Estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio, p1, p99, frames abaixo de 30/60fps
    31→     - AI pode usar browser_evaluate para análise programática
    32→     - Documentar em docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md
    33→
    34→3. **HARNESS-003** (Priority 1, blockedBy: PERF-001)
    35→   - Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais para incluir métricas de performance

    38→     - VISUAL: ler métricas do painel no screenshot (quick check)
    39→     - LOGGING: usar __PERF_START/STOP_SESSION__ (detailed check)
    40→   - Formato visual: 'PERF: FPS=60, MS=12.5, MB=45.2 - PASS'
    41→   - Formato log: 'PERF_LOG[test]: avgFPS=58.2, p1FPS=32.1, framesBelow30=12 - PASS'

   121→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅
   122→
   123→### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE
   124→
   125→**Objective:**
   126→Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.
   127→
   128→**Analysis:**
   129→Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:
   130→
   131→1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   132→   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   133→   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   134→   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌
   135→
   136→2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   137→   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   138→   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   139→   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   140→   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   141→   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3
   142→
   143→3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   144→   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   145→   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)
   146→
   147→**Solution:**
   148→Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
   149→- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
   150→- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
   151→- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds
   152→
   153→**Files Changed:**
   154→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (RowBackgrounds using InstancedMesh)
   155→- `features.json` (F116 passes: true)
   156→
   157→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   158→- ✅ MACRO view (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly with color gradient
   159→- ✅ MESO view (1.00x): Task blocks with alternating row backgrounds
   160→- ✅ MICRO view (2.25x): Task cards with labels, grid lines visible
   161→- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
   162→- ✅ No console errors
   163→
   164→**Performance Summary:**
   165→
   166→| Layer | Component | Before | After |
   167→|-------|-----------|--------|-------|
   168→| MesoLayer | RowBackgrounds | 50 draw calls | 1 draw call |
   169→| MesoLayer | TaskBlocks | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
   170→| MesoLayer | ConflictGlows | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
   171→| MicroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |
   172→| MacroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |
   173→
   174→**Next:** F123 (Minimap), F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation)

   178→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
   179→
   180→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
   181→
   182→**Problem:**
   183→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
   184→
   185→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   186→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
   187→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
   188→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
   189→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
   190→
   191→**Solution Implemented:**
   192→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
   193→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
   194→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   195→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   196→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   197→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   198→   - `handlePinchZoom`
   199→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   200→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
   201→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
   202→
   203→**Files Changed:**
   204→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
   205→- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)
   206→
   207→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   208→- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
   209→- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
   210→- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
   211→- ✅ No console errors
   212→

   231→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅
   232→
   233→### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE
   234→
   235→**Objective:**
   236→Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.
   237→
   238→**Implementation:**
   239→- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
   240→- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
   241→- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
   242→- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit
   243→
   244→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   245→- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
   246→- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
   247→- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
   248→- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
   249→- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
   250→- ✅ No console errors
   251→
   252→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
   253→
   254→**Objective:**
   255→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
   256→
   257→**Implementation:**
   258→
   259→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   260→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   261→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   262→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
   263→
   264→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   265→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   266→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   267→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   268→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
   269→
   270→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   271→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   272→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
   273→
   274→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   275→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   276→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
   277→
   278→**Constants Added:**
   279→```typescript
   280→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
   281→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
   282→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
   283→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
   284→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
   285→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
   286→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
   287→```
   288→
   289→**Files Changed:**
   290→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
   291→- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)
   292→

   294→- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
   295→- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
   296→- ✅ No console errors
   297→- ✅ Smooth zoom animation
   298→
   299→**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

   303→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅
   304→
   305→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
   306→
   307→**Objective:**
   308→Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.
   309→
   310→**Implementation:**
   311→
   312→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   313→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   314→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   315→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
   316→
   317→2. **Momentum Animation:**
   318→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   319→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   320→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   321→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
   322→
   323→3. **Integration:**
   324→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   325→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   326→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation
   327→
   328→**Constants Used:**
   329→```typescript
   330→MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
   331→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
   332→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
   333→```
   334→
   335→**Files Changed:**
   336→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
   337→- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)
   338→
   339→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   340→- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
   341→- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
   342→- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
   343→- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
   344→- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum
   345→
   346→**Known Issue (pre-existing):**
   347→- Reset View button doesn't fully reset camera position (separate bug, not F118)
   348→- React warning about setState during render (pre-existing, unrelated to momentum)
   349→
   350→**Next:** F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

   354→## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅
   355→
   356→### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation
   357→
   358→**Objective:**
   359→Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).
   360→
   361→**Research Conducted:**
   362→- Figma: Scroll wheel behavior, Ctrl+scroll zoom, middle-click pan, trackpad gestures
   363→- Google Maps: Momentum physics, gesture handling modes, camera easing (Quadratic.Out)
   364→- Apple Maps/iOS: Momentum for both pan AND zoom, double-tap gestures
   365→- Cross-platform scroll normalization: deltaMode, deltaY variations, Facebook's normalizeWheel pattern
   366→- Trackpad vs mouse detection: Event frequency heuristics
   367→- Pointer Events API: Pinch-to-zoom implementation pattern
   368→
   369→**Deliverables:**
   370→
   371→1. **Created `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md`:**
   372→   - Industry leader patterns (Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Canva)
   373→   - Complete mouse event reference (button values, drag patterns)
   374→   - Touch/Pointer events reference (pinch-to-zoom algorithm)
   375→   - Wheel event normalization (Facebook's algorithm)
   376→   - Momentum/inertia physics implementation
   377→   - Cross-platform considerations (macOS vs Windows scroll)
   378→   - Accessibility requirements (keyboard shortcuts, screen reader)
   379→   - Implementation recommendations for F118-F121
   380→
   381→2. **Updated Documentation:**
   382→   - `docs/README.md`: Added UX_RESEARCH.md to document index
   383→   - `features.json`: UX-AUDIT-001 passes: true
   384→
   385→**Key Findings:**
   386→
   387→| Pattern | Recommendation for MMS |
   388→|---------|------------------------|
   389→| Momentum | Friction 0.93-0.95, stop at velocity < 0.1 |
   390→| Zoom easing | quadOut over 250-350ms |
   391→| Double-click | 2x zoom, 350ms animation, cubicOut |
   392→| Scroll normalization | Use deltaY directly, clamp to ±0.5 per event |
   393→| Trackpad detection | Event frequency < 20ms = trackpad |
   394→
   395→**Sources:**
   396→- [MDN: Pinch zoom gestures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_events/Pinch_zoom_gestures)
   397→- [Google Maps: Camera Easing](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/move-camera-ease)
   398→- [Figma Help: Pan and zoom](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004414582-Pan-and-zoom-in-FigJam)
   399→- [Facebook normalizeWheel.js](https://github.com/facebookarchive/fixed-data-table/blob/master/src/vendor_upstream/dom/normalizeWheel.js)
   400→
   401→**Next:** F118 (Momentum panning), F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

   652→## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅
   653→
   654→### Performance Issues Resolved
   655→
   656→**Problems Fixed:**
   657→1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
   658→2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
   659→3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
   660→4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x
   661→
   662→**Optimizations Implemented:**
   663→
   664→#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
   665→- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
   666→- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
   667→- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)
   668→
   669→#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
   670→- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
   671→- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
   672→- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport
   673→
   674→#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
   675→- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
   676→- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
   677→- Only renders visible rows based on viewport
   678→
   679→#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
   680→- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
   681→- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
   682→- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport
   683→
   684→#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
   685→- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
   686→- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
   687→- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
   688→- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

   869→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)
   870→
   871→### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar
   872→
   873→**Symptoms:**
   874→- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
   875→- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
   876→- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)
   877→
   878→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   879→- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
   880→- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
   881→- Container div measured: 800x600
   882→- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
   883→- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
   884→- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070
   885→
   886→**Solution:**
   887→- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
   888→- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
   889→- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
   890→- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations
   891→
   892→**Files Changed:**
   893→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
   894→  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
   895→  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
   896→  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
   897→  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
   898→- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
   899→  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
   900→  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds
   901→
   902→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   903→- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
   904→- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
   905→- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
   906→- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
   907→- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
   908→- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
   909→- ✅ No console errors
   910→
   911→**Key Insight:**
   912→When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).
   913→
   914→**Features Updated:**
   915→- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
   916→- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
   917→- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)
   918→
   919→**Current State:**
   920→- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
   921→- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
   922→- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
   923→- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

  1174→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)
  1175→
  1176→### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness
  1177→
  1178→**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.
  1179→
  1180→**Root Cause Analysis:**
  1181→1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
  1182→2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
  1183→3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
  1184→4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand
  1185→
  1186→**Solution Implemented:**
  1187→
  1188→1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
  1189→   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
  1190→   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
  1191→   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
  1192→     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
  1193→     - Analyze why local docs failed
  1194→     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
  1195→     - Document in progress.txt
  1196→
  1197→2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
  1198→   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
  1199→   - Added official Anthropic link
  1200→   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
  1201→   - Removed duplicate section from line 293
  1202→
  1203→**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.
  1204→
  1205→**Sources:**
  1206→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
  1207→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
  1208→

  1326→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)
  1327→
  1328→### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks
  1329→
  1330→Based on:
  1331→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
  1332→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
  1333→
  1334→**Hooks Created:**
  1335→- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
  1336→- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
  1337→- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
  1338→- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
  1339→- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn
  1340→
  1341→**Architecture:**
  1342→```
  1343→PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
  1344→PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
  1345→Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
  1346→```
  1347→
  1348→**Commit:** 85485f8

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Harness Tasks Creation) - COMPLETE ✅

### Novas Tarefas Adicionadas ao Harness

**Contexto:**
Usuário solicitou criação de várias tarefas importantes para melhorar o harness e a qualidade do código.

**Tarefas Criadas:**

1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - EMERGENCIAL)
   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas
   - Auditar código, documentar usos, migrar estados críticos
   - Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra contra React hooks para estado crítico

2. **PERF-001** (Priority 0)
   - Adicionar ferramenta de métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real
   - Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada), stats-gl, stats.js
   - Painel deve mostrar: FPS, MS, MB, GPU time, draw calls, triangles
   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)
   - Toggle para mostrar/esconder (tecla 'P' ou botão)
   - **LOGGING DETALHADO** (adicionado após revisão):
     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console
     - window.__PERF_EXPORT__() para exportar log completo
     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()
     - Estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio, p1, p99, frames abaixo de 30/60fps
     - AI pode usar browser_evaluate para análise programática
     - Documentar em docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md

3. **HARNESS-003** (Priority 1, blockedBy: PERF-001)
   - Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais para incluir métricas de performance
   - Métricas obrigatórias: FPS >= 60 idle, >= 30 interação; MS <= 16.67ms
   - **DUAS abordagens complementares**:
     - VISUAL: ler métricas do painel no screenshot (quick check)
     - LOGGING: usar __PERF_START/STOP_SESSION__ (detailed check)
   - Formato visual: 'PERF: FPS=60, MS=12.5, MB=45.2 - PASS'
   - Formato log: 'PERF_LOG[test]: avgFPS=58.2, p1FPS=32.1, framesBelow30=12 - PASS'

4. **HARNESS-004** (Priority 1)
   - Migrar tarefas POC (F016-F028) do archive para fluxo normal
   - POC features NÃO são lixo - são features válidas a implementar
   - Remover sufixo -POC, atribuir prioridades adequadas
   - F024-POC removida (coberta por F116)

**Atualização de TECH-DEBT-001:**
- Relacionada a URGENT-001 - mesma iniciativa de eliminar React hooks
- @mms/toolkit já tem pattern ideal (useSyncExternalStore)
- cameraPosRef workaround é sintoma do problema

**Commit Realizado:**
- F124 comitada: `feat(canvas): F124 - Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation`

**Pesquisa de Ferramentas de Performance:**
- r3f-perf: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf (recomendada para R3F)
- stats-gl: https://github.com/RenaudRohlinger/stats-gl (WebGL2 GPU timing)
- stats.js: https://github.com/mrdoob/stats.js (básico)
- PerformanceMonitor (drei): adaptive quality based on FPS
- Spector.js: extensão Chrome/Firefox para debug WebGL

**Fontes:**
- https://r3f.docs.pmnd.rs/advanced/scaling-performance
- https://drei.docs.pmnd.rs/performances/performance-monitor
- https://sbcode.net/react-three-fiber/r3f-perf/

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F124 - Sidebar/Timeline Navigation) - COMPLETE ✅

### F124: Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Enable interactive navigation via sidebar (train list) and timeline header. Users can click items to jump directly to specific trains/dates.

**Implementation:**

1. **InfiniteCanvas Navigation API:**
   - Added `NavigationTarget` interface with trainIndex, dayOffset, optional zoom
   - Added `navigationTarget` and `onNavigationComplete` props
   - Navigation handler calculates target camera position and animates (350ms)
   - Uses existing `animateZoomTo()` function for smooth transitions

2. **Sidebar Interactive Train List:**
   - Train IDs now clickable with `cursor: pointer`
   - Hover effect: background changes to `#e1e8ed`
   - Tooltip: "Click to navigate to TRAIN-XXX"
   - Click navigates canvas to center on selected train

3. **Timeline Interactive Date Labels:**
   - Date labels now clickable with `cursor: pointer`
   - Hover effect: background highlight + darker text color
   - Tooltip: "Click to navigate to [date]"
   - Click navigates canvas to center on selected date

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (NavigationTarget, navigation handler)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (navigation state, handlers, sidebar onClick)
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (onDateClick prop, label interactivity)
- `features.json` (F124 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Sidebar shows "Click to navigate to TRAIN-XXX" tooltips
- ✅ Timeline shows "Click to navigate to [date]" tooltips
- ✅ Click TRAIN-040 → canvas centers on TRAIN-040 row
- ✅ Click Mar 2024 → canvas centers on March 2024
- ✅ Hover effects work (background highlight)
- ✅ No console errors

**Deferred to Future:**

**Next:** F123 (Minimap navigation), HARNESS-002 (reduce file sizes)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (RowBackgrounds using InstancedMesh)
- `features.json` (F116 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO view (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly with color gradient
- ✅ MESO view (1.00x): Task blocks with alternating row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO view (2.25x): Task cards with labels, grid lines visible
- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Summary:**

| Layer | Component | Before | After |
|-------|-----------|--------|-------|
| MesoLayer | RowBackgrounds | 50 draw calls | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | TaskBlocks | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MesoLayer | ConflictGlows | 1 draw call | 1 draw call |
| MicroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |
| MacroLayer | All | Already optimized | - |

**Next:** F123 (Minimap), F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### Harness Updates

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX
- Moved to archive.deferredFeatures

**F124 Added (Feature Request):**
- Sidebar and Timeline interactive navigation
- Click train IDs to navigate directly
- Click dates in timeline to navigate
- Search/filter for quick navigation
- Research best UX patterns (Figma, VS Code, design tools)

**Next:** F124 (Sidebar/Timeline navigation), F123 (Minimap), F116 (Performance)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**
- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Smooth zoom animation

**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

**Known Issue (pre-existing):**
- Reset View button doesn't fully reset camera position (separate bug, not F118)
- React warning about setState during render (pre-existing, unrelated to momentum)

**Next:** F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (UX-AUDIT-001 - UX Research) - COMPLETE ✅

### UX-AUDIT-001: UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation

**Objective:**
Research and document UX patterns from industry leaders to inform F118-F121 implementation (momentum panning, smooth zoom, double-click zoom, cross-platform scroll normalization).

**Research Conducted:**
- Figma: Scroll wheel behavior, Ctrl+scroll zoom, middle-click pan, trackpad gestures
- Google Maps: Momentum physics, gesture handling modes, camera easing (Quadratic.Out)
- Apple Maps/iOS: Momentum for both pan AND zoom, double-tap gestures
- Cross-platform scroll normalization: deltaMode, deltaY variations, Facebook's normalizeWheel pattern
- Trackpad vs mouse detection: Event frequency heuristics
- Pointer Events API: Pinch-to-zoom implementation pattern

**Deliverables:**

1. **Created `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md`:**
   - Industry leader patterns (Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Canva)
   - Complete mouse event reference (button values, drag patterns)
   - Touch/Pointer events reference (pinch-to-zoom algorithm)
   - Wheel event normalization (Facebook's algorithm)
   - Momentum/inertia physics implementation
   - Cross-platform considerations (macOS vs Windows scroll)
   - Accessibility requirements (keyboard shortcuts, screen reader)
   - Implementation recommendations for F118-F121

2. **Updated Documentation:**
   - `docs/README.md`: Added UX_RESEARCH.md to document index
   - `features.json`: UX-AUDIT-001 passes: true

**Key Findings:**

| Pattern | Recommendation for MMS |
|---------|------------------------|
| Momentum | Friction 0.93-0.95, stop at velocity < 0.1 |
| Zoom easing | quadOut over 250-350ms |
| Double-click | 2x zoom, 350ms animation, cubicOut |
| Scroll normalization | Use deltaY directly, clamp to ±0.5 per event |
| Trackpad detection | Event frequency < 20ms = trackpad |

**Sources:**
- [MDN: Pinch zoom gestures](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_events/Pinch_zoom_gestures)
- [Google Maps: Camera Easing](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/move-camera-ease)
- [Figma Help: Pan and zoom](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500004414582-Pan-and-zoom-in-FigJam)
- [Facebook normalizeWheel.js](https://github.com/facebookarchive/fixed-data-table/blob/master/src/vendor_upstream/dom/normalizeWheel.js)

**Next:** F118 (Momentum panning), F119 (Smooth zoom), F120 (Double-click zoom), F121 (Cross-platform scroll)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (QA-PLAN-001 - QA Testing Protocol) - COMPLETE ✅

## Session: 2025-12-16 (HARNESS-001 - Security & Playwright Headless) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-001: Configure Playwright MCP headless + Security Permissions

**Problem Identified:**
- Playwright MCP was running in headed mode, stealing focus during visual tests
- No protection against accessing personal directories (~/Desktop, ~/Documents, etc.)
- A `find ~` command triggered macOS access alerts

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Playwright MCP Headless Configuration:**
   - Created `.mcp.json` with `--headless` flag
   - Browser now runs invisibly without stealing focus
   - Added `.mcp.json` to `.gitignore` (user-specific config)

2. **Deny Permissions for Personal Directories:**
   - Added deny rules to `.claude/settings.local.json`

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### Performance Issues Resolved

**Problems Fixed:**
1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x

**Optimizations Implemented:**

#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)

#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport

#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
- Only renders visible rows based on viewport

#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport

#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportBounds, effectiveMinZoom, onMinZoomChange)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - ~50 labels visible (not 2000), grid lines visible
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ Dynamic MIN_ZOOM at 0.57x - content fills viewport, no excessive white space
- ✅ Sidebar readable at minimum zoom (not scrambled)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Impact:**
| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Grid Lines Draw Calls | 251 | 2 | 99.2% reduction |
| Row Background Draw Calls | 50 | 1 | 98% reduction |
| HTML Labels (MICRO) | ~2000 | max 50 | 97.5% reduction |
| Tasks Rendered | ~5500 all | ~50-200 visible | 96-99% reduction |
| MIN_ZOOM | 0.10x (white space) | 0.57x (fills viewport) | UX improvement |

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

**Current State:**
- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

**Commit:** 85485f8

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

# Harness Orchestrator - AI Instructions

## What is This?

This is a **sub-project** within the MMS monorepo. It's an AI orchestration system that manages multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel.

**IMPORTANT**: This is isolated from the main MMS canvas project. Do NOT modify files in `apps/canvas/` when working on this package.

---

## Harness Protocol

```
1. READ progress.txt → understand current state
2. READ features.json → find next feature (passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT the feature
4. TEST the implementation
5. UPDATE features.json → set passes: true
6. UPDATE progress.txt → add session notes
7. COMMIT changes to this package only
```

---

## Project Goal

Create an AI Project Manager that:
- Analyzes task dependencies from any harness features.json
- Identifies tasks that can run in parallel
- Dispatches multiple Claude agents simultaneously
- Tracks progress and handles failures
- Optimizes for quality, speed, and cost

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ORCHESTRATOR                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │  Dependency  │───▶│    Task      │───▶│    Agent     │  │
│  │    Graph     │    │  Scheduler   │    │    Pool      │  │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐                       │
│  │    State     │    │     AI       │                       │
│  │   Manager    │    │   Manager    │                       │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘                       │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Components

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|----------------|
| **DependencyGraph** | Parse blockedBy, build DAG, topological sort |
| **TaskScheduler** | Ready queue, priority scoring, critical path |
| **AgentPool** | Spawn Claude processes, manage lifecycle |
| **StateManager** | Atomic updates, file locking, recovery |
| **AIManager** | Smart decisions, model selection, quality review |

---

## Key Algorithms

### 1. Dependency Graph (DAG)

```typescript
// Build graph from features
features.forEach(f => {
  graph.addNode(f.id);
  if (f.blockedBy) {
    const blockers = Array.isArray(f.blockedBy) ? f.blockedBy : [f.blockedBy];
    blockers.forEach(b => graph.addEdge(b, f.id));
  }
});

// Find ready tasks (no pending deps)
function getReadyTasks() {
  return features.filter(f =>
    !f.passes &&
    getDependencies(f).every(dep => dep.passes)
  );
}
```

### 2. Critical Path

```typescript
// Longest path determines minimum completion time
function criticalPath(graph) {
  const distances = topologicalSort(graph).reduce((acc, node) => {
    const maxPredecessor = Math.max(
      0,
      ...getPredecessors(node).map(p => acc[p] + 1)
    );
    acc[node] = maxPredecessor;
    return acc;
  }, {});

  return Object.entries(distances)
    .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
}
```

### 3. File Locking

```typescript
// Prevent race conditions on features.json
import { lockfile } from 'proper-lockfile';

async function atomicUpdate(path, updater) {
  const release = await lockfile.lock(path);
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path));
    const updated = updater(data);
    writeFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2));
  } finally {
    release();
  }
}
```

---

## Directory Structure (Target)

```
packages/harness-orchestrator/
├── CLAUDE.md           # This file
├── features.json       # Feature definitions for this project
├── progress.txt        # Session progress log
├── package.json        # Dependencies
├── tsconfig.json       # TypeScript config
└── src/
    ├── index.ts        # CLI entry point
    ├── graph/
    │   ├── DependencyGraph.ts
    │   └── CriticalPath.ts
    ├── scheduler/
    │   ├── TaskScheduler.ts
    │   └── PriorityQueue.ts
    ├── agents/
    │   ├── AgentPool.ts
    │   └── ClaudeAgent.ts
    ├── state/
    │   ├── StateManager.ts
    │   └── FileLock.ts
    ├── ai/
    │   └── AIManager.ts
    └── cli/
        └── commands.ts
```

---

## Commands (Target)

```bash
# Run orchestration on main project
npx harness-orchestrator orchestrate ../../../features.json

# Analyze dependencies
npx harness-orchestrator analyze ../../../features.json

# Show status
npx harness-orchestrator status ../../../features.json

# Options
--max-agents 3      # Limit concurrent agents
--model sonnet      # Default model
--dry-run           # Show plan without executing
```

---

## Development Rules

1. **Isolation**: Only modify files in `packages/harness-orchestrator/`
2. **Testing**: Test with this package's own features.json first
3. **Commits**: Commit only this package's changes
4. **Dependencies**: Install deps here, not in root
5. **No Conflicts**: Don't touch apps/canvas or root features.json

---

## Quick Start for New Session

```bash
# 1. Navigate to package
cd packages/harness-orchestrator

# 2. Read current state
cat progress.txt
cat features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | .id'

# 3. Work on next feature
# ... implement ...

# 4. Update progress
# ... update progress.txt ...

# 5. Commit
git add packages/harness-orchestrator/
git commit -m "feat(orchestrator): description"
```

---

## Reference Implementation

See `scripts/harness-runner.js` for a simple sequential implementation. This orchestrator extends that concept with:
- Parallel execution
- Dependency analysis
- Intelligent scheduling
- State management

content.content:
1→# Orchestrator - Technical Notes
     2→
     3→Notes, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Claude Code CLI Notes
     8→
     9→### Key Learning
    10→The `claude` CLI has different modes that affect behavior significantly.
    11→
    12→#### Modes
    13→| Mode | Flag | Use Case |
    14→|------|------|----------|
    15→| Interactive | (default) | Human conversation, debugging |
    16→| Print | `-p` | Scripted execution, single task |
    17→| Continue | `--continue` | Resume last session |
    18→| Resume | `--resume <id>` | Resume specific session |
    19→
    20→#### Context Isolation (Critical for Quality)
    21→
    22→**Problem**: Running Claude indefinitely causes context buildup, which degrades response quality even with automatic compaction.
    23→
    24→**Solution**: Use `--no-session-persistence` flag to ensure each task runs in a **completely fresh context**.
    25→
    26→```bash
    27→# Fresh context per task (recommended for automation)
    28→claude -p --no-session-persistence "prompt"
    29→
    30→# This ensures:
    31→# 1. No session saved to disk
    32→# 2. Cannot be resumed
    33→# 3. Each execution starts with zero prior context
    34→# 4. Consistent quality regardless of previous tasks
    35→```
    36→
    37→**Architecture Pattern**:
    38→```
    39→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    40→│               Orchestrator Process                   │
    41→│  (runs continuously, coordinates everything)         │
    42→└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
    43→                   │ spawns
    44→        ┌──────────┼──────────┬──────────┐
    45→        ▼          ▼          ▼          ▼
    46→   ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐
    47→   │ Agent 1 │ │ Agent 2 │ │ Agent 3 │ │ Agent N │
    48→   │ Task A  │ │ Task B  │ │ Task C  │ │ Task X  │
    49→   │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │
    50→   │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │
    51→   └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘
    52→        │ exits     │ exits     │ exits     │ exits
    53→        ▼          ▼          ▼          ▼
    54→    [complete]  [complete] [complete] [complete]
    55→```
    56→
    57→Each agent:
    58→1. Starts with fresh context
    59→2. Reads progress.txt + features.json (state on disk)
    60→3. Implements ONE task
    61→4. Updates state files
    62→5. Exits (context discarded)
    63→
    64→#### Important Options
    65→```bash
    66→# Non-interactive execution (for automation)
    67→claude -p "prompt"
    68→
    69→# Fresh context, no persistence (RECOMMENDED)
    70→claude -p --no-session-persistence "prompt"
    71→
    72→# Limit spending
    73→claude --max-budget-usd 5 -p "prompt"
    74→
    75→# Specify model
    76→claude --model sonnet -p "prompt"
    77→
    78→# Output format for parsing
    79→claude -p --output-format json "prompt"
    80→```
    81→
    82→---
    83→
    84→## Parallelization Notes
    85→
    86→### Key Learning
    87→Not all "independent" tasks are safe to parallelize.
    88→
    89→#### Safe to Parallelize
    90→- Tasks that modify different files
    91→- Tasks that read but don't write shared state
    92→- Tasks with no file overlap
    93→
    94→#### NOT Safe to Parallelize
    95→- Tasks that both modify features.json
    96→- Tasks that might conflict in git
    97→- Tasks that share build/test infrastructure
    98→
    99→#### Solution: File Locking
   100→```javascript
   101→// Use proper-lockfile for atomic updates
   102→import { lock } from 'proper-lockfile';
   103→
   104→const release = await lock('features.json');
   105→try {
   106→  // Read, modify, write
   107→} finally {
   108→  await release();
   109→}
   110→```
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## Process Management Notes
   115→
   116→### Key Learning
   117→Child processes need careful handling.
   118→
   119→#### Correct Usage
   120→```javascript
   121→const claude = spawn('claude', args, {
   122→  cwd: ROOT_DIR,
   123→  stdio: 'inherit', // Show output in terminal
   124→  shell: true,      // Required for some environments
   125→});
   126→
   127→// Handle all events
   128→claude.on('error', handleError);
   129→claude.on('close', handleClose);
   130→claude.on('exit', handleExit);
   131→```
   132→
   133→#### Common Pitfalls
   134→- Don't assume process exits cleanly - always handle errors
   135→- Use `shell: true` for PATH resolution
   136→- Capture stderr separately if you need error logs
   137→- Set timeouts to prevent hanging processes
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## DAG/Dependency Notes
   142→
   143→### Key Learning
   144→The `blockedBy` field can be a string or array.
   145→
   146→#### Correct Parsing
   147→```javascript
   148→function getDependencies(feature) {
   149→  if (!feature.blockedBy) return [];
   150→  return Array.isArray(feature.blockedBy)
   151→    ? feature.blockedBy
   152→    : [feature.blockedBy];
   153→}
   154→```
   155→
   156→#### Cycle Detection
   157→A harness with cycles will deadlock. Always validate:
   158→```javascript
   159→function detectCycles(graph) {
   160→  const visited = new Set();
   161→  const stack = new Set();
   162→
   163→  function dfs(node) {
   164→    visited.add(node);
   165→    stack.add(node);
   166→
   167→    for (const dep of graph.getDependents(node)) {
   168→      if (stack.has(dep)) return true; // Cycle!
   169→      if (!visited.has(dep) && dfs(dep)) return true;
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    stack.delete(node);
   173→    return false;
   174→  }
   175→
   176→  return [...graph.nodes].some(n => !visited.has(n) && dfs(n));
   177→}
   178→```
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## Error Solutions
   183→
   184→### Error: "ENOENT: no such file or directory" on file lock
   185→**Cause**: Trying to lock a file that doesn't exist
   186→**Solution**: Create file first or use `realpath: false` option
   187→
   188→### Error: Child process hangs indefinitely
   189→**Cause**: Claude waiting for input or stuck in loop
   190→**Solution**: Add timeout and kill on timeout
   191→```javascript
   192→const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
   193→  claude.kill('SIGTERM');
   194→}, 10 * 60 * 1000); // 10 minutes
   195→
   196→claude.on('close', () => clearTimeout(timeout));
   197→```
   198→
   199→### Error: features.json corrupted after parallel writes
   200→**Cause**: Race condition - two processes read/write simultaneously
   201→**Solution**: Use file locking (proper-lockfile)
   202→
   203→### Error: Git merge conflicts from parallel branches
   204→**Cause**: Multiple agents committing to same branch
   205→**Solution**: Use feature branches, merge sequentially
   206→
   207→---
   208→
   209→## Performance Notes
   210→
   211→### Optimal Agent Count
   212→| System | Recommended | Notes |
   213→|--------|-------------|-------|
   214→| Local dev | 2-3 | CPU bound |
   215→| CI/CD | 3-5 | I/O bound |
   216→| API limits | Check rate limits | Anthropic has per-minute limits |
   217→
   218→### Cost Optimization
   219→- Use `haiku` for simple/quick tasks
   220→- Use `sonnet` for normal tasks
   221→- Reserve `opus` for complex/critical tasks
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Useful Commands
   226→
   227→```bash
   228→# Test orchestrator locally
   229→node packages/harness-orchestrator/src/index.js analyze features.json
   230→
   231→# Check for pending tasks
   232→jq '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | .id' features.json
   233→
   234→# Find blocked features
   235→jq '.features[] | select(.blockedBy != null and .passes == false)' features.json
   236→
   237→# Count parallelizable tasks
   238→node -e "
   239→  const f = require('./features.json');
   240→  const ready = f.features.filter(t =>
   241→    !t.passes &&
   242→    (!t.blockedBy || f.features.find(b => b.id === t.blockedBy)?.passes)
   243→  );
   244→  console.log('Ready:', ready.map(t => t.id));
   245→"
   246→```
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Anthropic Official Harness Protocol (O014 Research)
   251→
   252→> Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents
   253→> Researched: 2025-12-16
   254→
   255→### Core Problem
   256→
   257→Long-running agents must work in discrete sessions. Each new session begins with **NO memory** of previous sessions. This is like engineers working in shifts where each new engineer has no memory of the previous shift.
   258→
   259→### Solution: Two-Agent Architecture
   260→
   261→Anthropic's solution uses two specialized agents:
   262→
   263→1. **Initializer Agent** (runs ONCE on first session):
   264→   - Sets up the environment
   265→   - Creates `init.sh` script for dev server startup
   266→   - Creates `claude-progress.txt` for session logs
   267→   - Creates initial git commit
   268→   - Generates comprehensive feature list (JSON format)
   269→
   270→2. **Coding Agent** (runs in ALL subsequent sessions):
   271→   - Makes incremental progress
   272→   - Works on ONE feature per session
   273→   - Leaves clean, merge-ready code
   274→   - Updates artifacts for next session
   275→
   276→### Session Workflow (Critical!)
   277→
   278→Each session follows this startup sequence:
   279→
   280→```
   281→1. Run `pwd` to verify working directory
   282→2. Read git logs and progress files for context
   283→3. Execute init.sh to start the development server
   284→4. Perform basic functionality testing
   285→5. Read the feature list and select highest-priority incomplete feature
   286→6. Implement ONE feature incrementally
   287→7. Commit to git with descriptive messages
   288→8. Update claude-progress.txt before session end
   289→```
   290→
   291→### The `harness continue` Command
   292→
   293→This is the **core command** that should:
   294→1. Detect if this is first run (no progress.txt) → Run Initializer Agent
   295→2. If progress.txt exists → Run Coding Agent
   296→3. Coding Agent reads state, picks next feature, works on it, commits
   297→4. Each invocation = one feature completed
   298→
   299→### Key Files in Harness
   300→
   301→| File | Purpose | Who Creates |
   302→|------|---------|-------------|
   303→| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history log | Initializer |
   304→| `features.json` | Feature list with `passes` status | Initializer |
   305→| `init.sh` | Dev server startup script | Initializer |
   306→| `CLAUDE.md` | Project instructions for agent | Manual or Initializer |
   307→
   308→### Feature JSON Format
   309→
   310→```json
   311→{
   312→  "category": "functional",
   313→  "description": "Feature description",
   314→  "steps": ["step1", "step2", ...],
   315→  "passes": false
   316→}
   317→```
   318→
   319→**Important**: Coding agents can ONLY modify the `passes` field. This prevents accidental deletions.
   320→
   321→### Failure Modes & Solutions
   322→
   323→| Problem | Solution |
   324→|---------|----------|
   325→| Declaring project complete prematurely | Comprehensive feature list with explicit "failing" status |
   326→| Leaving undocumented, buggy code | Git commits + progress file updates at session end |
   327→| Marking features done without testing | Browser automation (Playwright MCP) required |
   328→| Time spent understanding project state | init.sh + progress file reading at startup |
   329→
   330→### Incremental Progress Pattern
   331→
   332→**CRITICAL**: Work on ONE feature per session.
   333→
   334→Why:
   335→- Prevents context overflow
   336→- Ensures clean, merge-ready code
   337→- Each session leaves artifacts for the next
   338→- No half-implemented, undocumented features
   339→
   340→### Visual Testing is MANDATORY
   341→
   342→From Anthropic's research:
   343→> "Claude failed to identify non-working features without explicit UI testing prompts"
   344→
   345→Solution: Browser automation (Playwright MCP) for end-to-end verification, not just unit tests.
   346→
   347→### Claude Agent SDK Integration
   348→
   349→The SDK provides:
   350→- Built-in tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch
   351→- Sessions: Can resume with `resume: sessionId`
   352→- Hooks: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd
   353→- MCP integration: Playwright, databases, APIs
   354→- Subagents: Task tool for delegation
   355→
   356→### Key SDK Options
   357→
   358→```typescript
   359→import { query } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";
   360→
   361→// Resume a session
   362→for await (const message of query({
   363→  prompt: "Continue working on the feature",
   364→  options: { resume: sessionId }
   365→})) {
   366→  // ...
   367→}
   368→
   369→// With hooks
   370→for await (const message of query({
   371→  prompt: "Fix the bug",
   372→  options: {
   373→    hooks: {
   374→      PostToolUse: [{
   375→        matcher: "Edit|Write",
   376→        hooks: [{ type: "command", command: "echo 'file modified'" }]
   377→      }]
   378→    }
   379→  }
   380→})) {
   381→  // ...
   382→}
   383→```
   384→
   385→---
   386→
   387→## Resources
   388→
   389→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   390→- [Claude Agent SDK Documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview)
   391→- [Claude Code CLI Docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude-code)
   392→- [proper-lockfile npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/proper-lockfile)
   393→- [DAG algorithms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Directed_acyclic_graph)
   394→- [Process management in Node.js](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html)

file.content:
# Orchestrator - Technical Notes

Notes, gotchas, and solutions discovered during development. Read this to avoid repeating past mistakes.

---

## Claude Code CLI Notes

### Key Learning
The `claude` CLI has different modes that affect behavior significantly.

#### Modes
| Mode | Flag | Use Case |
|------|------|----------|
| Interactive | (default) | Human conversation, debugging |
| Print | `-p` | Scripted execution, single task |
| Continue | `--continue` | Resume last session |
| Resume | `--resume <id>` | Resume specific session |

#### Context Isolation (Critical for Quality)

**Problem**: Running Claude indefinitely causes context buildup, which degrades response quality even with automatic compaction.

**Solution**: Use `--no-session-persistence` flag to ensure each task runs in a **completely fresh context**.

```bash
# Fresh context per task (recommended for automation)
claude -p --no-session-persistence "prompt"

# This ensures:
# 1. No session saved to disk
# 2. Cannot be resumed
# 3. Each execution starts with zero prior context
# 4. Consistent quality regardless of previous tasks
```

**Architecture Pattern**:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               Orchestrator Process                   │
│  (runs continuously, coordinates everything)         │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                   │ spawns
        ┌──────────┼──────────┬──────────┐
        ▼          ▼          ▼          ▼
   ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐
   │ Agent 1 │ │ Agent 2 │ │ Agent 3 │ │ Agent N │
   │ Task A  │ │ Task B  │ │ Task C  │ │ Task X  │
   │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │ │ FRESH   │
   │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │ │ CONTEXT │
   └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘ └────┬────┘
        │ exits     │ exits     │ exits     │ exits
        ▼          ▼          ▼          ▼
    [complete]  [complete] [complete] [complete]
```

Each agent:
1. Starts with fresh context
2. Reads progress.txt + features.json (state on disk)
3. Implements ONE task
4. Updates state files
5. Exits (context discarded)

#### Important Options
```bash
# Non-interactive execution (for automation)
claude -p "prompt"

# Fresh context, no persistence (RECOMMENDED)
claude -p --no-session-persistence "prompt"

# Limit spending
claude --max-budget-usd 5 -p "prompt"

# Specify model
claude --model sonnet -p "prompt"

# Output format for parsing
claude -p --output-format json "prompt"
```

---

## Parallelization Notes

### Key Learning
Not all "independent" tasks are safe to parallelize.

#### Safe to Parallelize
- Tasks that modify different files
- Tasks that read but don't write shared state
- Tasks with no file overlap

#### NOT Safe to Parallelize
- Tasks that both modify features.json
- Tasks that might conflict in git
- Tasks that share build/test infrastructure

#### Solution: File Locking
```javascript
// Use proper-lockfile for atomic updates
import { lock } from 'proper-lockfile';

const release = await lock('features.json');
try {
  // Read, modify, write
} finally {
  await release();
}
```

---

## Process Management Notes

### Key Learning
Child processes need careful handling.

#### Correct Usage
```javascript
const claude = spawn('claude', args, {
  cwd: ROOT_DIR,
  stdio: 'inherit', // Show output in terminal
  shell: true,      // Required for some environments
});

// Handle all events
claude.on('error', handleError);
claude.on('close', handleClose);
claude.on('exit', handleExit);
```

#### Common Pitfalls
- Don't assume process exits cleanly - always handle errors
- Use `shell: true` for PATH resolution
- Capture stderr separately if you need error logs
- Set timeouts to prevent hanging processes

---

## DAG/Dependency Notes

### Key Learning
The `blockedBy` field can be a string or array.

#### Correct Parsing
```javascript
function getDependencies(feature) {
  if (!feature.blockedBy) return [];
  return Array.isArray(feature.blockedBy)
    ? feature.blockedBy
    : [feature.blockedBy];
}
```

#### Cycle Detection
A harness with cycles will deadlock. Always validate:
```javascript
function detectCycles(graph) {
  const visited = new Set();
  const stack = new Set();

  function dfs(node) {
    visited.add(node);
    stack.add(node);

    for (const dep of graph.getDependents(node)) {
      if (stack.has(dep)) return true; // Cycle!
      if (!visited.has(dep) && dfs(dep)) return true;
    }

    stack.delete(node);
    return false;
  }

  return [...graph.nodes].some(n => !visited.has(n) && dfs(n));
}
```

---

## Error Solutions

### Error: "ENOENT: no such file or directory" on file lock
**Cause**: Trying to lock a file that doesn't exist
**Solution**: Create file first or use `realpath: false` option

### Error: Child process hangs indefinitely
**Cause**: Claude waiting for input or stuck in loop
**Solution**: Add timeout and kill on timeout
```javascript
const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
  claude.kill('SIGTERM');
}, 10 * 60 * 1000); // 10 minutes

claude.on('close', () => clearTimeout(timeout));
```

### Error: features.json corrupted after parallel writes
**Cause**: Race condition - two processes read/write simultaneously
**Solution**: Use file locking (proper-lockfile)

### Error: Git merge conflicts from parallel branches
**Cause**: Multiple agents committing to same branch
**Solution**: Use feature branches, merge sequentially

---

## Performance Notes

### Optimal Agent Count
| System | Recommended | Notes |
|--------|-------------|-------|
| Local dev | 2-3 | CPU bound |
| CI/CD | 3-5 | I/O bound |
| API limits | Check rate limits | Anthropic has per-minute limits |

### Cost Optimization
- Use `haiku` for simple/quick tasks
- Use `sonnet` for normal tasks
- Reserve `opus` for complex/critical tasks

---

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Test orchestrator locally
node packages/harness-orchestrator/src/index.js analyze features.json

# Check for pending tasks
jq '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | .id' features.json

# Find blocked features
jq '.features[] | select(.blockedBy != null and .passes == false)' features.json

# Count parallelizable tasks
node -e "
  const f = require('./features.json');
  const ready = f.features.filter(t =>
    !t.passes &&
    (!t.blockedBy || f.features.find(b => b.id === t.blockedBy)?.passes)
  );
  console.log('Ready:', ready.map(t => t.id));
"
```

---

## Anthropic Official Harness Protocol (O014 Research)

> Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents
> Researched: 2025-12-16

### Core Problem

Long-running agents must work in discrete sessions. Each new session begins with **NO memory** of previous sessions. This is like engineers working in shifts where each new engineer has no memory of the previous shift.

### Solution: Two-Agent Architecture

Anthropic's solution uses two specialized agents:

1. **Initializer Agent** (runs ONCE on first session):
   - Sets up the environment
   - Creates `init.sh` script for dev server startup
   - Creates `claude-progress.txt` for session logs
   - Creates initial git commit
   - Generates comprehensive feature list (JSON format)

2. **Coding Agent** (runs in ALL subsequent sessions):
   - Makes incremental progress
   - Works on ONE feature per session
   - Leaves clean, merge-ready code
   - Updates artifacts for next session

### Session Workflow (Critical!)

Each session follows this startup sequence:

```
1. Run `pwd` to verify working directory
2. Read git logs and progress files for context
3. Execute init.sh to start the development server
4. Perform basic functionality testing
5. Read the feature list and select highest-priority incomplete feature
6. Implement ONE feature incrementally
7. Commit to git with descriptive messages
8. Update claude-progress.txt before session end
```

### The `harness continue` Command

This is the **core command** that should:
1. Detect if this is first run (no progress.txt) → Run Initializer Agent
2. If progress.txt exists → Run Coding Agent
3. Coding Agent reads state, picks next feature, works on it, commits
4. Each invocation = one feature completed

### Key Files in Harness

| File | Purpose | Who Creates |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history log | Initializer |
| `features.json` | Feature list with `passes` status | Initializer |
| `init.sh` | Dev server startup script | Initializer |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Project instructions for agent | Manual or Initializer |

### Feature JSON Format

```json
{
  "category": "functional",
  "description": "Feature description",
  "steps": ["step1", "step2", ...],
  "passes": false
}
```

**Important**: Coding agents can ONLY modify the `passes` field. This prevents accidental deletions.

### Failure Modes & Solutions

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Declaring project complete prematurely | Comprehensive feature list with explicit "failing" status |
| Leaving undocumented, buggy code | Git commits + progress file updates at session end |
| Marking features done without testing | Browser automation (Playwright MCP) required |
| Time spent understanding project state | init.sh + progress file reading at startup |

### Incremental Progress Pattern

**CRITICAL**: Work on ONE feature per session.

Why:
- Prevents context overflow
- Ensures clean, merge-ready code
- Each session leaves artifacts for the next
- No half-implemented, undocumented features

### Visual Testing is MANDATORY

From Anthropic's research:
> "Claude failed to identify non-working features without explicit UI testing prompts"

Solution: Browser automation (Playwright MCP) for end-to-end verification, not just unit tests.

### Claude Agent SDK Integration

The SDK provides:
- Built-in tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch
- Sessions: Can resume with `resume: sessionId`
- Hooks: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, SessionEnd
- MCP integration: Playwright, databases, APIs
- Subagents: Task tool for delegation

### Key SDK Options

```typescript
import { query } from "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk";

// Resume a session
for await (const message of query({
  prompt: "Continue working on the feature",
  options: { resume: sessionId }
})) {
  // ...
}

// With hooks
for await (const message of query({
  prompt: "Fix the bug",
  options: {
    hooks: {
      PostToolUse: [{
        matcher: "Edit|Write",
        hooks: [{ type: "command", command: "echo 'file modified'" }]
      }]
    }
  }
})) {
  // ...
}
```

---

## Resources

- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Agent SDK Documentation](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview)
- [Claude Code CLI Docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude-code)
- [proper-lockfile npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/proper-lockfile)
- [DAG algorithms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Directed_acyclic_graph)
- [Process management in Node.js](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html)

    23→
    24→### Architecture Decisions
    25→
    26→#### Why Isolated Package?
    27→- Avoid dependency conflicts with main project
    28→- Can install own deps (commander, chalk, ora)
    29→- Separate harness tracking
    30→- Won't interfere with parallel agents on main project
    31→
    32→#### Core Components Planned
    33→```
    34→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    35→│                    ORCHESTRATOR                              │
    36→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    37→│                                                              │
    38→│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
    39→│  │  Dependency  │───▶│    Task      │───▶│    Agent     │  │
    40→│  │    Graph     │    │  Scheduler   │    │    Pool      │  │
    41→│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
    42→│         │                   │                   │           │
    43→│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
    44→│                             ▼                               │
    45→│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
    46→│                    │    State     │                         │
    47→│                    │   Manager    │                         │
    48→│                    └──────────────┘                         │
    49→│                             │                               │
    50→│                             ▼                               │
    51→│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
    52→│                    │     AI       │                         │
    53→│                    │   Manager    │                         │
    54→│                    └──────────────┘                         │
    55→│                                                              │
    56→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    57→```
    58→
    59→#### Parallelization Strategy
    60→1. Build DAG from features.json blockedBy
    61→2. Find all nodes with no pending dependencies (ready queue)
    62→3. Execute ready tasks in parallel (up to max-agents limit)
    63→4. On completion: update state, unlock dependents, repeat
    64→
    65→#### Critical Path Optimization
    66→- Calculate longest path through DAG
    67→- Prioritize tasks on critical path
    68→- Reduces total completion time
    69→
